features.name: Build & Expand Town Buildings
features.description: Construct and upgrade town buildings to unlock new hero classes and income streams.
features.implementation_notes: Building placement via B key + number selection + click. All 9 building types exist with costs matching GDD. Upgrade system via U key.

features.name: Attract Heroes via Buildings
features.description: Heroes arrive at the town gate when the right buildings exist (e.g., inn attracts warriors, wizard tower attracts mages).
features.implementation_notes: hero_attraction_system spawns heroes near the building that attracted them every 15-30 seconds. Inn->Warrior/Rogue, Temple->Healer, WizardTower->Mage, Barracks->Warrior/Archer, Market->Rogue.
features.discrepancy: Heroes spawn near the attracting building instead of at a designated town gate as per GDD. Also Market attracting Rogues is not in GDD.

features.name: Set Bounties to Influence Behavior
features.description: Open the Bounty Board and place gold rewards on monster dens, ruins, resource nodes, or boss threats. Heroes autonomously evaluate bounties.
features.implementation_notes: BountyBoard resource with 4 types (Monster, Exploration, Objective, Resource). Auto-bounty for dens, manual placement via Q key + click. Heroes score bounties based on personality, distance, danger, class affinity.

features.name: Manage Gold Economy
features.description: Collect taxes from buildings and market trade. Balance spending between bounties, building upgrades, and emergency reserves.
features.implementation_notes: tax_collection_system handles per-building income scaled by tier and road bonus. Bounty payouts, construction costs, repair costs all deduct gold.

features.name: Survive Threats & Boss Raids
features.description: Goblins, bandits, and monsters attack in waves, especially at night. Boss events trigger periodically.
features.implementation_notes: monster_den_spawn_system, enemy_ai_system, boss_raid_system, era_siege_system all handle threat generation. Night boosts spawn rates by 50%.

features.name: Plan Road Networks
features.description: Plan road networks to connect buildings and speed up hero movement.
features.implementation_notes: R key + click places road tiles at 5g each. RoadNetwork resource tracks positions. 30% speed boost on roads. Connection bonuses for Market and Blacksmith.

features.name: Expand Map Perimeter
features.description: Expand the map perimeter to reveal new monster zones and resource deposits.
features.implementation_notes: E key expands fog radius by 100 units per expansion. Rank-gated max expansions. Spawns new dens and resource nodes in revealed area.

features.name: Hero AI Decision-Making
features.description: Each hero evaluates bounty value, perceived danger, current needs, personality, and distance every few seconds to decide their next action.
features.implementation_notes: hero_ai_system runs on decision_timer (2-3s). Scores bounties using gold_reward, distance penalty, danger factor by personality, class affinity, night penalty. Priority ordering: low HP -> low morale at night -> current bounty -> available bounties -> nearby enemies -> wander.

features.name: Bounty Board with 4 Types
features.description: Bounty Board lists active bounties categorised by type: Monster, Exploration, Objective, Resource.
features.implementation_notes: BountyBoard resource manages all bounties. auto_bounty_system creates Monster bounties for dens. resource_bounty_system creates Resource bounties. objective_bounty_system creates defend-building bounties. Manual Exploration bounties via Q+click.

features.name: Dynamic Bounty Amount Adjustment
features.description: Players can adjust bounty amounts dynamically to shift hero focus when a new threat emerges.
features.implementation_notes: No UI or system exists to modify bounty reward amounts after creation.
features.discrepancy: Manual bounties are always fixed at 30g. Auto-bounties scale with threat tier but player cannot adjust them.

features.name: Bounty ROI Visibility
features.description: The bounty amount is never wasted gold - player always has visibility into their ROI before committing.
features.implementation_notes: No ROI or cost-benefit display exists in the bounty board UI.
features.discrepancy: Bounty board shows reward and danger level but no ROI analysis or expected completion probability.

features.name: Guild Assignment System
features.description: Assign heroes to guilds or leave them free-roaming for different behavioral patterns.
features.implementation_notes: No guild concept exists in code. All heroes are free-roaming.
features.discrepancy: GDD Step 2 mentions guilds. No implementation at all.

features.name: Multi-Hero Bounty Stacking
features.description: All bounties on the boss stack, and all hero classes can participate. Multiple heroes should be able to work on the same bounty.
features.implementation_notes: BountyBoard.assign_bounty() only allows one hero per bounty. Boss/siege bounties cannot have multiple heroes assigned.
features.discrepancy: GDD explicitly says 'All bounties on the boss stack, all hero classes can participate' but code limits to 1 hero per bounty.

features.name: Five Hero Classes
features.description: Warrior (tank), Archer (ranged DPS), Mage (AoE), Rogue (scout/assassin), Healer (support).
features.implementation_notes: All 5 classes defined in HeroClass enum with unique base stats, colors, display names.

features.name: Hero Personalities
features.description: Brave (ignores danger), Cautious (demands higher pay), Greedy (prioritises gold), Loyal (lower threshold).
features.implementation_notes: HeroPersonality enum with 4 variants. Randomly assigned on hero creation. Affects bounty scoring in hero_ai_system.

features.name: Warrior Fortify Aura
features.description: Fortify: temporary damage reduction aura. Warriors near allies grant them damage reduction.
features.implementation_notes: warrior_fortify_aura_system grants 20% damage reduction per nearby warrior (within 100 units), capped at 40%. Applied via fortify_reduction field on HeroStats.

features.name: Archer Volley (AoE Arrow Rain)
features.description: Volley: AoE arrow rain on clustered enemies. Should damage multiple enemies in an area.
features.implementation_notes: hero_attack_system only gives Archer a 1.2x single-target bonus at range > 80. No actual AoE mechanic - only hits one enemy at a time.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'AoE arrow rain on clustered enemies' but code only does single-target with a range bonus multiplier. No area-of-effect damage.

features.name: Mage Arcane Surge (Multi-Target Blast)
features.description: Arcane Surge: channelled multi-target blast. Should hit multiple enemies simultaneously.
features.implementation_notes: hero_attack_system only gives Mage a flat 1.3x damage multiplier on single-target attacks. No multi-target or channeling mechanic.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'channelled multi-target blast' but code only does 1.3x single-target damage. No AoE, no channeling.

features.name: Rogue Backstab (Crit on Unaware)
features.description: Backstab: critical hit on unaware enemies. Should only crit when enemy hasn't detected the rogue.
features.implementation_notes: hero_attack_system gives Rogue a flat 30% crit chance (2x damage) on every attack. No awareness check on enemy.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'critical hit on unaware enemies' but code does random 30% crit on all attacks regardless of enemy awareness state.

features.name: Rogue Stealth Mechanic
features.description: Rogue enters camps unseen, performs priority kills. Stealth state allows undetected movement.
features.implementation_notes: No stealth system exists. Rogues are visible and targeted like any other hero.
features.discrepancy: GDD combat style is 'Stealth - enters camps unseen, priority kills'. No stealth mechanic at all.

features.name: Healer Sanctuary (Heal Pulse + Revive)
features.description: Sanctuary: healing pulse that revives fallen heroes. Should be an AoE heal with revive capability.
features.implementation_notes: healer_system heals one nearby ally per tick (single target, below 80% HP threshold). No revive mechanic, no pulse/AoE healing.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'healing pulse that revives fallen heroes' but code does single-target healing only with no revive. Dead heroes auto-revive on a timer regardless of healer.

features.name: Healer Follow-Party Behavior
features.description: Healer combat style is 'Passive - follows party, heals low-HP allies'. Should attach to groups.
features.implementation_notes: Healers use the same AI as all other heroes. No party-following logic exists.
features.discrepancy: Healers act independently rather than following other heroes as a support unit.

features.name: Hero XP & Leveling
features.description: Heroes gain XP from completing bounties, clearing camps, and surviving combat. Level up with stat boosts.
features.implementation_notes: hero_progression_system checks xp >= xp_to_next. Grants +10 max_hp, +2 attack, +1 defense, +1 speed per level. XP from bounties (25 bonus) and enemy kills (shared 50%).

features.name: Perk Points Every 5 Levels
features.description: Every 5 levels, a hero earns a Perk Point for class-specific upgrades.
features.implementation_notes: Auto-applied in hero_progression_system when level % 5 == 0. Warrior: +20hp/+3def, Archer: +5atk/+20range, Mage: +8atk, Rogue: +5spd/+4atk, Healer: +15hp.
features.discrepancy: Perks are auto-applied rather than player-chosen. GDD implies player choice ('spend on class-specific upgrades').

features.name: Legendary Heroes at Level 10+
features.description: Veteran heroes (level 10+) become Legendary - golden border portrait, significantly boosted stats.
features.implementation_notes: hero_progression_system sets is_legendary=true at level 10. Grants 1.3x max_hp, 1.25x attack, 1.2x defense. Alert message shown.
features.discrepancy: Golden border portrait visual is not implemented. Stat boost works but visual distinction is missing.

features.name: Hero Death & Recovery Bounty
features.description: If a hero dies, they are incapacitated for a period. If the player neglects to pay their recovery bounty, the hero is permanently lost.
features.implementation_notes: Dead state with 30s respawn_timer exists. recovery_bounty_system creates an Objective bounty near dead heroes. However: (a) hero auto-revives on timer regardless of bounty, (b) completing the recovery bounty does nothing special, (c) no permanent death if bounty is neglected.
features.discrepancy: Recovery bounty is cosmetic - it has no actual effect. Heroes always auto-revive. No permanent death consequence for neglecting the bounty.

features.name: Hero Decision Timer (2s)
features.description: Heroes reconsider their actions every few seconds based on AI evaluation.
features.implementation_notes: HeroDecisionTimer component, resets to 2.0 + random 1.0s after each decision cycle in hero_ai_system.

features.name: Hero Service Timer (7s)
features.description: Heroes perform service/economic interactions (shopping, praying, resting) on a 7-second timer separate from combat decisions.
features.implementation_notes: No service_timer exists. Only decision_timer is implemented. Hero economic actions are part of the main AI loop.
features.discrepancy: CLAUDE.md mentions 'service_timer (every 7s)' for economic interactions. Not implemented.

features.name: Patrol Task
features.description: Heroes patrol designated areas, moving between waypoints autonomously.
features.implementation_notes: No Patrol state in HeroState enum. Heroes only wander randomly when idle.
features.discrepancy: CLAUDE.md lists Patrol as a HeroTask but it doesn't exist in the code.

features.name: PrayAtTemple Task
features.description: Heroes visit the temple to pray, restoring morale. Part of hero autonomous life simulation.
features.implementation_notes: No PrayAtTemple state. Morale restoration only happens during Resting state at inn. Temple morale aura is passive/global.
features.discrepancy: GDD says heroes 'pray at temples' but no temple-visiting behavior exists.

features.name: Shopping Task
features.description: Heroes visit the market to shop, spending carried gold. Part of hero autonomous life.
features.implementation_notes: HeroState::Shopping variant exists in code but is never entered by any AI logic. Completely unused.
features.discrepancy: Shopping state is defined but no AI path leads to it. Heroes never shop at the market.

features.name: ExploreRuins Task
features.description: Heroes explore ruins tiles, discovering treasure and lore.
features.implementation_notes: No ExploreRuins state. No ruins tile type despite being listed in CLAUDE.md tile kinds.
features.discrepancy: CLAUDE.md mentions Ruins as a tile kind and ExploreRuins as a task. Neither implemented.

features.name: GatherResource Task
features.description: Heroes explicitly gather from resource nodes as a dedicated task.
features.implementation_notes: Resource nodes activate by hero proximity (60 unit radius). No dedicated GatherResource state - heroes don't intentionally seek resource nodes.
features.discrepancy: Resources activate passively when heroes happen to be nearby. No intentional gathering behavior.

features.name: DefendTown Task
features.description: Heroes take a defensive stance near town buildings during threats.
features.implementation_notes: No DefendTown state. Heroes attack nearby enemies reactively but don't adopt defensive positioning.
features.discrepancy: CLAUDE.md lists DefendTown as a task. No implementation.

features.name: EscortMerchant Task
features.description: Heroes escort merchant caravans to protect them from bandits along trade routes.
features.implementation_notes: Merchant component exists with escort-related fields but no AI task links heroes to merchant entities. Merchants travel unescorted.
features.discrepancy: GDD mentions escort bounties as an Objective subtype. Merchant entities exist but heroes never escort them.

features.name: LayLow Task
features.description: Heroes hide when overwhelmed or morale is critically low, avoiding combat entirely.
features.implementation_notes: No LayLow state. Low morale heroes just stay in Resting state at inn.
features.discrepancy: CLAUDE.md lists LayLow as a task for cowardly/overwhelmed heroes.

features.name: All 9 Building Types
features.description: TownHall, Inn, Market, Temple, GuardTower, WizardTower, Blacksmith, Alchemist, Barracks - all with GDD costs.
features.implementation_notes: BuildingType enum with 9 variants. Costs match GDD exactly (Inn:150, Market:200, Temple:250, GuardTower:300, Blacksmith:350, Alchemist:300, WizardTower:400, Barracks:450).

features.name: Building Unlock Prerequisites (Tier-Based)
features.description: Buildings unlock based on other building tier requirements: Inn needs Town Hall T1, Temple needs Town Hall T2, Guard Tower needs Inn T1, Wizard Tower needs Temple T1, Blacksmith needs Inn T2, Alchemist needs Temple T2, Barracks needs Guard Tower T2.
features.implementation_notes: Buildings are gated by KingdomRank instead of building tier prerequisites. KingdomRank::available_buildings() returns lists based on rank (Hamlet, Village, etc). No tier-dependency checks exist.
features.discrepancy: GDD has a clear tech-tree based on building tiers. Code uses kingdom rank as a flat gate. For example, GDD says Guard Tower requires Inn T1 but code just requires Village rank.

features.name: Building Upgrade Tiers (0-3)
features.description: Buildings upgrade through tiers: T1 improves base function, T2 new passive ability, T3 major transformation.
features.implementation_notes: Building.tier field (0-3). U key + mouse proximity to upgrade. Cost scales via building_type.upgrade_cost(tier). HP increases 1.3x per tier.

features.name: Guard Tower Auto-Attack
features.description: Guard Towers provide automated arrow defense against nearby enemies.
features.implementation_notes: guard_tower_attack_system fires every 2s. Range: 150 + tier*50. Damage: 15 + tier*10. Hits one enemy per tick.

features.name: Guard Tower Enemy Slow Effect
features.description: Guard Tower slows enemy advances near the town core in addition to dealing damage.
features.implementation_notes: No slow/debuff mechanic exists. Guard towers only deal damage.
features.discrepancy: GDD says Guard Tower 'slows enemy advances near the town core'. No speed debuff on enemies.

features.name: Building Destruction & Auto-Repair
features.description: Destroyed buildings cost gold to repair. Emergency repairs should be player-triggered.
features.implementation_notes: building_repair_system auto-repairs every 5 seconds if gold available. Cost is 50% of build cost. No player control over repair timing or priority.
features.discrepancy: GDD implies player-controlled emergency repairs. Code auto-repairs without player input. Also no 200g treasury reserve enforcement.

features.name: Grid-Based Building Placement
features.description: The town is built on a grid-based map. Buildings snap to grid positions with overlap detection.
features.implementation_notes: Buildings are placed freely at cursor world position. No grid snapping, no overlap detection, no placement validation.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'grid-based map'. CLAUDE.md specifies '20x15 tile grid, TILE_SIZE=40.0'. Code has free-form placement.

features.name: Inn Tier Bonuses (Heal Speed)
features.description: Inn T1: heals 50% faster. Inn T2: heals 100% faster.
features.implementation_notes: building_bonuses_system sets inn_heal_speed to 1.5 (T1) or 2.0 (T2). Used in hero_rest_system to multiply heal rate.

features.name: Market Tier Bonuses (Trade Income)
features.description: Market T1: extra trade gold. Market T2: trade caravans arrive weekly with rare items.
features.implementation_notes: building_bonuses_system sets market_trade_bonus +10 (T1) or +30 (T1+T2). Applied to merchant arrival income. No 'rare items' or weekly caravans mechanic.
features.discrepancy: Market T2 'trade caravans with rare items' not implemented. Only flat gold bonus.

features.name: Temple Tier Bonuses (Morale Aura)
features.description: Temple provides morale boost aura to nearby heroes. T3 Cathedral generates 50g/day pilgrim income.
features.implementation_notes: temple_morale_aura set to 2.0 (T1) or 5.0 (T2). Used in hero_rest_system. temple_pilgrim_income=50.0 at T3 via cathedral_income_system.

features.name: Blacksmith ATK/DEF Bonus Application
features.description: Blacksmith boosts hero attack and armor. Should apply flat ATK/DEF bonus to all heroes.
features.implementation_notes: building_bonuses_system calculates blacksmith_atk_bonus (3.0/6.0) and blacksmith_def_bonus (2.0/4.0). These values are stored in BuildingBonuses resource but NO system ever reads or applies them to hero HeroStats.
features.discrepancy: Blacksmith bonus is calculated but never applied. Heroes receive zero benefit from Blacksmith despite the building existing and having tier bonuses computed.

features.name: Wizard Tower Research Bonus Application
features.description: Wizard Tower unlocks arcane research upgrades, boosting mage damage.
features.implementation_notes: building_bonuses_system calculates wizard_research_bonus (1.2 at T1, 1.5 at T2). This multiplier is stored but NO system ever applies it to mage damage in hero_attack_system or anywhere else.
features.discrepancy: Wizard research bonus is calculated but never applied. Mages get zero benefit from Wizard Tower tier upgrades.

features.name: Alchemist Recovery Speed
features.description: Alchemist reduces hero recovery time when incapacitated.
features.implementation_notes: alchemist_recovery_speed set to 1.5 (T1) or 2.0 (T2). apply_building_bonuses_system subtracts extra time from death respawn_timer.

features.name: Barracks Hero Cap Bonus
features.description: Barracks increases the hero party cap allowing more heroes in the kingdom.
features.implementation_notes: barracks_hero_cap_bonus set to 2 (T1) or 5 (T2). Added to kingdom.rank.max_heroes() in hero_attraction_system.

features.name: Equipment Crafting at Blacksmith
features.description: Blacksmith enables equipment crafting for heroes to get permanent gear upgrades.
features.implementation_notes: No crafting system, no equipment/inventory system exists.
features.discrepancy: GDD says Blacksmith 'enables equipment crafting'. No crafting exists.

features.name: Potion Crafting at Alchemist
features.description: Alchemist crafts potions that heroes can use for healing or buffs.
features.implementation_notes: No potion system exists. Alchemist only provides passive recovery speed bonus.
features.discrepancy: GDD says Alchemist 'Crafts potions for heroes'. No potion items exist.

features.name: Barracks Squad Formation Bounties
features.description: Barracks enables squad formation bounties where multiple heroes work together.
features.implementation_notes: No squad or formation system exists. All heroes act independently.
features.discrepancy: GDD says Barracks 'enables squad formation bounties'. No squad mechanic.

features.name: Road Placement
features.description: Players place paved roads to connect buildings. Drag-and-drop with snap-to-grid.
features.implementation_notes: R key + left click places road tiles (5g each). Small brown sprites. No drag-and-drop, no snap-to-grid.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'Drag-and-drop road placement with snap-to-grid for comfortable mobile use'. Code uses keyboard+click with free placement.

features.name: Road Speed Boost (30%)
features.description: Paved roads increase hero and merchant movement speed by 30%.
features.implementation_notes: RoadNetwork.speed_multiplier() returns 1.3 when hero is within 12px of a road tile. Applied in hero_movement_system.

features.name: Road Connection Bonuses
features.description: Roads connecting buildings improve efficiency: market tax bonus and blacksmith craft speed.
features.implementation_notes: road_connection_bonus_system uses BFS through road tiles to check if buildings are connected. Market connections: +10% tax per link. Blacksmith connections: +15% ATK/DEF per link.

features.name: Bridge Building over Rivers
features.description: Players can build bridges to cross rivers and open new map sectors.
features.implementation_notes: No bridge system, no river/water obstacles, no sector-crossing mechanics. Water texture is loaded but not used as an obstacle.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'Players can build bridges to cross rivers and open new map sectors'. Not implemented.

features.name: Property Tax Income
features.description: Each building generates daily gold income based on its tier.
features.implementation_notes: tax_collection_system calculates per-building tax_income(tier) every frame, scaled by time and speed. Road connection multiplier applied.

features.name: Market Trade (Merchant Caravans)
features.description: Merchants arrive periodically during daytime and pay a trade fee proportional to town level.
features.implementation_notes: merchant_spawn_system spawns merchants twice per game-day during daytime. Trade value scales with kingdom rank. merchant_movement_system moves them to town center and pays gold on arrival.

features.name: Bounty Tax (10% Return)
features.description: A small percentage of completed bounties is returned to the treasury as a transaction fee.
features.implementation_notes: bounty_payout_system deducts full reward, gives hero 90%, returns 10% tax to treasury.

features.name: Event/Milestone Gold Rewards
features.description: Completing kingdom milestones grants one-time gold bonuses.
features.implementation_notes: milestone_system awards gold for: first den cleared (+100), village rank (+150), town rank (+250), city rank (+400), first legendary (+200), 10 heroes (+150).

features.name: Resource Nodes (Mine/Lumber)
features.description: Mining camps and lumber mills generate passive income if heroes keep them safe.
features.implementation_notes: 4 resource nodes spawned at startup (2 mines, 2 lumber). resource_node_system activates when hero within 60 units. Generates gold every 5s while active. resource_bounty_system creates bounties for inactive nodes.

features.name: Hero Signing Bonus
features.description: Some high-tier heroes require a signing bonus to join the kingdom.
features.implementation_notes: No signing bonus system. All heroes arrive for free when attracted by buildings.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'some high-tier heroes require a signing bonus'. Not implemented.

features.name: Treasury Reserve Warning (200g)
features.description: Never let treasury reach zero. Keep at least 200 gold in reserve. Alert player when below threshold.
features.implementation_notes: No reserve warning system. Auto-repair spends gold freely. No bankruptcy alert until heroes start leaving.
features.discrepancy: GDD economy tip says keep 200g reserve. No warning or enforcement in code.

features.name: Income Breakdown UI
features.description: Detailed income-per-minute breakdown showing sources of gold income.
features.implementation_notes: UI shows total income_per_minute but no breakdown by source (tax vs trade vs bounties etc).
features.discrepancy: Only aggregate income shown, no per-source breakdown.

features.name: Goblin Rabble
features.description: Weak individually; spawn in large numbers from goblin camps. High bounty efficiency for low-level heroes.
features.implementation_notes: EnemyType::Goblin with 30hp, 8atk, 2def. Spawns from MonsterDen. 15xp, 10g reward.

features.name: Bandit Gang (Target Caravans)
features.description: Organised attackers that target the market and road caravans. Mid-tier threat.
features.implementation_notes: EnemyType::Bandit with 50hp, 14atk. enemy_ai_system has special logic where bandits prioritize buildings. However bandits don't specifically target caravans/merchants.
features.discrepancy: Bandits target buildings generally but don't specifically hunt merchant caravans on roads as GDD describes.

features.name: Cave Troll (Destroy Guard Towers)
features.description: Tanky, slow monsters that destroy guard towers on contact. Require a mixed hero party.
features.implementation_notes: EnemyType::Troll with 150hp, 25atk, 15def, speed 15. Attacks nearest target generically like other enemies.
features.discrepancy: GDD says trolls 'destroy guard towers on contact'. Code has no special guard tower targeting or instant-destroy-on-contact for trolls.

features.name: Monster Dens (Persistent Spawn Points)
features.description: Persistent spawn points that generate monsters until cleared by heroes.
features.implementation_notes: MonsterDen component with spawn_timer, spawn_interval, max_spawned. monster_den_spawn_system spawns enemies periodically. den_destruction_system allows heroes to damage and destroy dens.

features.name: Dungeon Lord Boss
features.description: End-of-era Dungeon Lord boss marches on the town. All bounties stack, all hero classes participate.
features.implementation_notes: EnemyType::BossWarlord exists (500hp, 40atk, 20def). boss_raid_system spawns at City/Kingdom rank every 5 game-days. era_siege_system spawns mixed enemies including BossWarlord. No 'Dungeon Lord' entity type - reuses BossWarlord.
features.discrepancy: Boss raids are random periodic events, not specifically the end-of-era Dungeon Lord. No dedicated Dungeon Lord enemy type with unique sprite. Boss spawns from boss_raid_system AND era_siege_system with overlapping concerns.

features.name: Werewolf (Night-Only)
features.description: Werewolves only spawn at night and despawn at dawn. Fast and dangerous.
features.implementation_notes: EnemyType::Werewolf with 90hp, 22atk, speed 50. night_enemy_spawn_system spawns 1-2 night creatures every 20s at night. night_enemy_despawn_system removes them at dawn.

features.name: Shadow Bandit (Night-Only)
features.description: Shadow Bandits only spawn at night. Stealthy attackers.
features.implementation_notes: EnemyType::ShadowBandit with 60hp, 20atk, speed 45. Same spawn/despawn logic as Werewolf.

features.name: Threat Escalation (Weekly)
features.description: Every in-game week a threat zone goes unaddressed, it escalates one tier. Goblin camp -> Stronghold -> Warlord.
features.implementation_notes: threat_escalation_system increments weeks_unaddressed every game-day. At 7 weeks: tier+1, more spawns, faster interval. Goblins upgrade to GoblinElite at tier 2.
features.discrepancy: GDD describes three-stage escalation: 'camp -> Stronghold -> Warlord that raids town'. Code only has tier number increment and GoblinElite upgrade. No 'Stronghold' visual change, no 'Warlord' that launches targeted town raids.

features.name: Dynamic Storm/Weather Spawning
features.description: Higher-difficulty threats spawn during stormy weather events.
features.implementation_notes: No weather system exists. Threat difficulty only varies by day/night.
features.discrepancy: GDD mentions 'stormy weather events' increasing spawn difficulty. Not implemented.

features.name: Day/Night Cycle (8-Minute Day)
features.description: One full in-game day passes every 8 minutes. Cycle: Dawn, Day, Dusk, Night.
features.implementation_notes: day_length=480.0s. day_night_cycle_system calculates day_progress and time_of_day. Dawn:0-15%, Day:15-55%, Dusk:55-70%, Night:70-100%.

features.name: Night Overlay Visual
features.description: Day/night lighting via real-time colour overlay with soft torch halos.
features.implementation_notes: NightOverlay sprite (2000x2000) with ambient_color() changing alpha/color by time. Dawn: warm yellow, Day: transparent, Dusk: orange, Night: dark blue.
features.discrepancy: Basic color overlay works. No soft torch halos around buildings at night as GDD art direction specifies.

features.name: Night Threat Boost (+50%)
features.description: Threat spawn rates increase by 50% at night.
features.implementation_notes: GameTime.threat_multiplier() returns 1.5 at Night, 1.25 at Dusk. Applied to den spawn intervals in monster_den_spawn_system.

features.name: Low Morale Heroes Refuse Night Missions
features.description: Heroes with low morale refuse to leave the inn after dark unless the bounty is very high.
features.implementation_notes: hero_ai_system: if night and morale < 30, hero enters Resting state. Night penalty 0.7x on bounty scores for non-Brave heroes.

features.name: Torch/Light Defense Bonus
features.description: Buildings within town's light radius receive a small defense bonus at night.
features.implementation_notes: torch_defense_system grants +3 defense to heroes within 100 units of non-destroyed buildings during night. Buff tracked via HashSet to apply/remove correctly.

features.name: Speed Toggle (1x, 2x, Pause)
features.description: Players can run time at 1x, 2x, or pause during planning phases.
features.implementation_notes: speed_control_system supports 1x, 2x, 3x via number keys and Space for pause. 3x mode exists but is not in GDD.
features.discrepancy: Code adds 3x speed which GDD doesn't mention. GDD says only '1x, 2x, or pause'. Minor discrepancy - 3x is an addition, not a bug.

features.name: Kingdom Ranks (5 Tiers)
features.description: Hamlet -> Village -> Town -> City -> Kingdom. Each unlocks new zones, enemy types, building blueprints.
features.implementation_notes: KingdomRank enum with 5 variants. kingdom_progression_system promotes based on building_count and hero_count thresholds. Each rank has max_heroes, max_expansions, available_buildings.

features.name: Zone Unlocks per Rank
features.description: Each rank unlocks specific map zones: Village->Forest, Town->Mountain, City->Dungeon, Kingdom->Endgame.
features.implementation_notes: Map expansion exists but zones are generic. No Forest/Mountain/Dungeon zone types with unique content tied to rank.
features.discrepancy: GDD specifies rank-specific zone unlocks (Forest zone at Village, Mountain at Town, etc). Code just expands a radius.

features.name: Score Calculation (Wealth Included)
features.description: Score based on kingdom wealth, heroes alive, and buildings standing.
features.implementation_notes: kingdom.score = hero_count*100 + buildings_count*200 + era_day*10. Gold/wealth is not factored in.
features.discrepancy: GDD says score includes 'kingdom wealth'. Code omits gold from score calculation.

features.name: Era System (30-60 Day Runs)
features.description: Each game is an Era lasting 30-60 in-game days, ending with a Dungeon Lord siege. Surviving completes the era.
features.implementation_notes: EraState with era_length_days=45. era_siege_system triggers when era_day >= era_length_days. 5 waves of enemies. Legacy points awarded on completion. Era counter increments.
features.discrepancy: Era siege works mechanically but no meaningful reset happens after era completion. No score screen, no fresh start.

features.name: Era Completion Score Screen
features.description: Surviving the siege completes the Era and awards a score based on kingdom wealth, heroes alive, and buildings standing.
features.implementation_notes: Era completion only shows an alert message. No score screen, no detailed breakdown.
features.discrepancy: No era completion UI screen with score breakdown.

features.name: Legacy Points Spending UI
features.description: Era completion awards Legacy Points spent on permanent bonuses (+10% tax income, heroes start at level 2).
features.implementation_notes: LegacyUpgrades struct exists with tax_bonus_pct, hero_start_level, building_hp_bonus_pct, bounty_cost_reduction. Legacy points are awarded after siege. BUT: No UI to spend points, and NO system reads LegacyUpgrades to apply any bonuses. The struct is completely inert.
features.discrepancy: Legacy system is architecturally present but functionally dead. Points are earned but can never be spent, and upgrades are never applied to gameplay.

features.name: Roguelite Fresh Start
features.description: Each new Era begins fresh but carries forward Legacy upgrades, giving long-term progression.
features.implementation_notes: After era completion, game just continues. No reset of buildings, heroes, gold, or map state. No fresh start mechanic.
features.discrepancy: GDD says each era begins fresh. Code just increments era counter and continues.

features.name: Challenge Eras
features.description: Special challenge Eras with unique modifiers: 'No Inns', 'Double Bounties', 'Permanent Death'.
features.implementation_notes: No challenge mode system exists.
features.discrepancy: GDD lists 3 challenge modes. None implemented.

features.name: Gold Counter with Income
features.description: Always visible gold counter with income-per-minute indicator in top bar.
features.implementation_notes: update_gold_ui shows 'Gold: X (+Y/min)' using GoldText component. Updated every frame from GameEconomy.

features.name: Day/Night Clock Display
features.description: Simple sun/moon arc shows the current time of day at a glance.
features.implementation_notes: update_day_night_ui shows 'Day X - Dawn/Day/Dusk/Night'. Text color changes by time. No sun/moon arc visual.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'sun/moon arc'. Code shows text only, no graphical arc indicator.

features.name: Hero Panel
features.description: Collapsible side panel showing all active heroes, their status, and current task.
features.implementation_notes: update_hero_panel_ui shows hero count, class breakdown, and first 5 hero details (class, level, HP, state). Panel is always visible, not collapsible.
features.discrepancy: Panel is not collapsible/expandable. Shows max 5 heroes with '...' truncation.

features.name: Alert/Toast System
features.description: Non-intrusive toast notifications for threat escalations, building completions, bounty payouts.
features.implementation_notes: GameAlerts resource with timed messages (5s display). update_alerts_ui shows most recent alert at bottom-center. Used extensively throughout all systems.

features.name: Build Menu
features.description: Building selection menu accessed via keyboard with building list and costs.
features.implementation_notes: B key opens build menu showing available buildings with number key selection. Number key selects building type, then click to place. Right-click to cancel.

features.name: Bounty Board UI Panel
features.description: Bounty Board accessible via floating action button. Shows categorized bounty list.
features.implementation_notes: Q key toggles BountyBoardUi panel. Shows active bounties with type icon, reward, danger, status. Click map to place exploration bounty (30g).
features.discrepancy: No floating action button - uses Q key. Bounty board is text-only, no visual bounty cards. Only places Exploration bounties manually.

features.name: Click-to-Inspect (Info Cards)
features.description: Long-press on any building or hero shows contextual info card with quick-action buttons.
features.implementation_notes: I key + left click shows detailed info for heroes, buildings, or enemies via alert text. Info includes stats, state, tier, etc.
features.discrepancy: Uses keyboard shortcut (I+click) instead of long-press. Shows info as text alert, not a visual card. No quick-action buttons.

features.name: Mobile-First Touch Controls
features.description: One-handed play on smartphone. All tap targets minimum 48x48pt. Portrait and landscape adaptive layouts.
features.implementation_notes: Entire UI is keyboard/mouse driven (WASD, B, U, E, Q, I, R keys). No touch input handling. Desktop-only layout.
features.discrepancy: GDD is mobile-first. Current implementation is desktop-only with keyboard shortcuts.

features.name: Kingdom Rank & Score Display
features.description: Display current kingdom rank, score, and era number.
features.implementation_notes: update_kingdom_rank_ui shows 'Rank | Score: X | Era Y' in top bar.

features.name: Speed Display
features.description: Show current game speed and pause state.
features.implementation_notes: update_speed_ui shows '1x', '2x', '3x', or '||' for paused.

features.name: Auto-Save System (30s)
features.description: Game state is saved every 30 seconds automatically. No risk of progress loss.
features.implementation_notes: No save system of any kind. No serialization, no SaveSnapshot, no autosave timer. CLAUDE.md mentions AutoSaveState and SaveSnapshot but they don't exist in code.
features.discrepancy: GDD requires auto-save every 30s. CLAUDE.md describes a save system architecture. Nothing is implemented.

features.name: Save/Load from JSON
features.description: Full game state saved to JSON file via serde. SaveSnapshot captures all resources, heroes, buildings, enemies.
features.implementation_notes: CLAUDE.md describes serde_json SaveSnapshot with version field. No implementation exists. Components don't derive Serialize/Deserialize.
features.discrepancy: Complete save/load system is missing.

features.name: Offline Progress
features.description: Kingdom continues earning passive income and heroes keep working when the player is away.
features.implementation_notes: No offline progress calculation. Game only runs while active.
features.discrepancy: GDD requires offline progress. Not implemented.

features.name: Grid-Based Tile Map (20x15)
features.description: 20x15 tile grid with TILE_SIZE=40.0, origin at (-380,260). Tile kinds: Grass, Road, Forest, Mountain, Water, Ruins. 4 sectors with reveal requirements.
features.implementation_notes: Ground is rendered with random grass sprites in a large area. No MapState resource, no TileState, no grid system, no tile kinds. Free-form world coordinates used everywhere.
features.discrepancy: CLAUDE.md describes a detailed grid-based map with sectors. Code has no grid system at all.

features.name: Fog of War
features.description: Dark fog tiles that clear as heroes explore and player expands territory.
features.implementation_notes: FogOfWar resource tracks revealed_radius and explored_areas. fog_of_war_system reveals tiles near heroes. Black semi-transparent tiles cover unexplored areas.

features.name: Map Expansion System
features.description: E key expands map perimeter, revealing new zones with content. Rank-gated and gold-costed.
features.implementation_notes: map_expansion_system: E key, cost scales with expansion count, rank limits max expansions. Spawns new den and resource node in newly revealed ring. Named zones (Outer Fields, Dark Forest, etc).

features.name: Sprite Assets for All Entities
features.description: Distinct sprites for all hero classes, enemy types, buildings, and environment.
features.implementation_notes: SpriteAssets loaded at startup. Heroes use leader sprites (Daniel, Robin, Raja, Rollo, Sami). Enemies use creep sprites with tints. Buildings use Blue/Red building atlases. Trees and grass from environment assets.

features.name: Hero Animation Sets
features.description: Heroes have distinct 32x32 sprites with full walk, attack, idle, and rest animation sets.
features.implementation_notes: SpriteAnimation component exists with frame cycling. Most heroes use single-frame sprites. Archer uses 4-frame atlas. Goblin uses 4-frame atlas. Basic sprite flipping for direction.
features.discrepancy: Most heroes are single-frame static sprites. No walk/attack/idle/rest animation states per the GDD.

features.name: Golden Border on Legendary Heroes
features.description: Legendary heroes' portraits gain a golden border visual effect.
features.implementation_notes: is_legendary flag is set at level 10. Alert shown. No visual change to the hero sprite.
features.discrepancy: No golden border or visual distinction for legendary heroes.

features.name: Animated Building Interiors
features.description: Animated building interiors visible through windows - flickering fires, moving silhouettes.
features.implementation_notes: Buildings are static single-frame sprites. No interior animation.
features.discrepancy: No building interior animations.

features.name: Torch Halo Visual at Night
features.description: Soft torch halos around buildings at night creating a light radius effect.
features.implementation_notes: Night overlay is a single global color overlay. No per-building light halos.
features.discrepancy: GDD art direction specifies torch halos. Only flat overlay exists.

features.name: Adaptive Music (Day/Night)
features.description: Calm acoustic lute during daytime; tense orchestral strings at night.
features.implementation_notes: No audio system at all. Zero sound files or audio playback code.
features.discrepancy: No audio of any kind in the game.

features.name: Class-Specific Sound Effects
features.description: Each hero class has unique footstep and combat sound effects.
features.implementation_notes: No audio system exists.
features.discrepancy: No audio.

features.name: Town Ambience by Building Count
features.description: Town ambience layered by building count: merchants calling, blacksmith hammering, temple bells.
features.implementation_notes: No audio system exists.
features.discrepancy: No audio.

features.name: Boss Raid Siren Horn
features.description: Distinct dramatic horn that alerts the player to begin bounty preparation for boss raids.
features.implementation_notes: Boss raids only show text alert. No audio cue.
features.discrepancy: No audio.

features.name: Cosmetic Hero Skins
features.description: Alternate visual skins for each hero class (purely visual, no stat changes).
features.implementation_notes: No skin system, no IAP, no cosmetic unlocks.
features.discrepancy: No monetization system exists.

features.name: Kingdom Themes
features.description: Alternative art packs for buildings (desert, frost, volcanic biome themes).
features.implementation_notes: No theme system exists.
features.discrepancy: No monetization system exists.

features.name: Speed-Up Pass (Permanent 3x)
features.description: One-time purchase that permanently unlocks 3x speed. Currently 3x is free.
features.implementation_notes: 3x speed is freely available via keyboard. GDD positions it as a paid convenience unlock.
features.discrepancy: 3x speed is already free in the game. GDD says it should be a paid unlock.

features.name: Battle Pass (Seasonal Cosmetics)
features.description: Seasonal story content with cosmetic rewards earned through normal gameplay.
features.implementation_notes: No battle pass or seasonal content system.
features.discrepancy: No monetization system exists.

features.name: Hero Personality-Driven Behavior
features.description: Heroes have individual personalities affecting their bounty decisions and behavior.
features.implementation_notes: 4 personality types with distinct bounty scoring: Brave ignores danger, Cautious penalizes danger, Greedy ignores danger (only gold), Loyal moderate caution.

features.name: Heroes Refuse Night Missions
features.description: Heroes with low morale can refuse night missions, creating emergent drama.
features.implementation_notes: Morale check at night (< 30 = refuse). Night bounty score penalty for non-Brave heroes.

features.name: Heroes Leave if Idle
features.description: Idle heroes without bounties or bankrupt kingdoms cause heroes to leave after a period.
features.implementation_notes: hero_idle_leave_system tracks idle time. After 120s of no bounties or low morale + bankrupt, hero despawns. Alert shown.

features.name: Heroes Fall in Love at Inn
features.description: Heroes can fall in love at the inn, creating relationship dynamics and emergent stories.
features.implementation_notes: No relationship system exists.
features.discrepancy: GDD unique selling point mentions heroes 'fall in love at the inn'. Not implemented.

features.name: Heroes Flee in Panic
features.description: Heroes flee in panic during overwhelming battles, creating emergent storytelling.
features.implementation_notes: No flee/panic mechanic. Heroes fight until dead or HP threshold triggers inn-seeking.
features.discrepancy: GDD mentions heroes can 'flee in panic'. No flee behavior implemented.

features.name: Push Notifications
features.description: Optional alerts for boss raid warnings, building completions, and treasury criticals.
features.implementation_notes: Desktop app, no push notification system.
features.discrepancy: Mobile feature. Not applicable to desktop build.

features.name: Tutorial / Onboarding
features.description: Core loop is learned in 2 minutes. Tutorial guides new players through build, bounty, survive cycle.
features.implementation_notes: No tutorial system. Only a controls help bar at screen bottom.
features.discrepancy: GDD says 'core loop is learned in 2 minutes'. No guided tutorial.

features.name: Starting Heroes Configuration
features.description: Heroes should arrive via buildings, not spawn at game start. Initial heroes could be a design choice but differs from pure GDD attraction model.
features.implementation_notes: spawn_initial_heroes creates 2 Warriors and 1 Archer at fixed positions at startup, bypassing the building attraction system entirely.
features.discrepancy: GDD implies all heroes arrive through building attraction. Code pre-spawns 3 heroes. May be intentional for gameplay feel but deviates from GDD.

features.name: Camera System
features.description: Pan and zoom camera for viewing the kingdom map.
features.implementation_notes: WASD/arrow keys for panning, scroll wheel for zoom, +/- keys for zoom. Scale clamped 0.2-3.0. Speed scales with zoom level.

features.name: Hero Morale Decay System
features.description: Hero morale decays over time, faster at night. Resting restores morale.
features.implementation_notes: hero_morale_system: 0.3/s decay during day, 1.0/s at night. Resting adds 5.0/s. Morale clamped 0-100.
